steps.action: Kizomba is a social dance and music genre that o with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Kizomba is a social dance and music genre that o with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: Kizomba is characterized by a slower, romantic,
steps.text: Kizomba is characterized by a slower, romantic, more sensuous rhythm than the traditional Angolan semba music.

steps.action: Kizomba music emerged as a fusion of Angolan Sem
steps.text: Kizomba music emerged as a fusion of Angolan Semba with Antillean zouk, and further Angolan music influences: It slowed down the cadence of songs and added a stronger bass line to the composition of instruments.

steps.action: In the 1980s, when the actual kizomba music was
steps.text: In the 1980s, when the actual kizomba music was pioneered and got more and more popular, Angolans started to adapt their semba steps according to the tempo and flavour of the Kizomba beats.

steps.action: As such Kizomba (dance) has essentially remained
steps.text: As such Kizomba (dance) has essentially remained to many Angolans to what is referred to as "Semba Social", only that it is danced to a spectrum of various music genre such as semba, kizomba or zouk.

steps.action: The emphasis is "Semba social" as Semba is often
steps.text: The emphasis is "Semba social" as Semba is often misinterpreted as "Semba Show" which is a style of Semba that is characterized with fast semba or zouk music alongside various dance tricks.

steps.action: The Kizomba dance is a couple dance, in which th
steps.text: The Kizomba dance is a couple dance, in which the torso and right arm of the leader will guide the follower across the dance floor.

steps.action: It is the goal to synchronize perfectly as a cou
steps.text: It is the goal to synchronize perfectly as a couple with the music and express it through elegant footwork, smooth body movement, and attitude, called Ginga (for women) and Banga (for men).

steps.action: It is danced accompanied by a partner, very smoo
steps.text: It is danced accompanied by a partner, very smoothly, slowly and sensuously, and with neither tightness nor rigidity.

steps.action: There are frequent simultaneous hip rotations co
steps.text: There are frequent simultaneous hip rotations coordinated between dance partners, particularly in the quieter refrains of the music.

steps.action: Key left the dance-pop/R&B songs to introduce th
steps.text: Key left the dance-pop/R&B songs to introduce the kizomba in Brazil.

steps.action: Every year, different Latin dance festivals are
steps.text: Every year, different Latin dance festivals are organized and presented such as Shanghai Bachata/Kizomba Festival.

steps.action: Kizomba is a national heritage of Angola and mea
steps.text: Kizomba is a national heritage of Angola and means "party" in Kimbundu.

steps.action: Eduardo Paím is internationally recognized as th
steps.text: Eduardo Paím is internationally recognized as the "father/creator of kizomba music", as he and his band were taking a major role in the development of the music style creation.

steps.action: Most kizomba songs are sung in Angolan Portugues
steps.text: Most kizomba songs are sung in Angolan Portuguese/Portuguese.

steps.action: This was as a result of the development of moder
steps.text: This was as a result of the development of modern Angolan music which is attributed to the musical group Ngola Ritmos.

steps.action: After gaining independence from Portugal, Angola
steps.text: After gaining independence from Portugal, Angola entered a civil war in 1975 that lasted to 2002.

steps.action: As few semba music was being released during thi
steps.text: As few semba music was being released during this early period, Angolans started to look else where for musicality such as the Caribbean.

steps.action: As Kizomba is essentially "Semba Social", that l
steps.text: As Kizomba is essentially "Semba Social", that left room for "Semba Show" to be marketed as Semba by Angolan teachers outside of Angola.

steps.action: The São Tomean kizomba music is very similar to
steps.text: The São Tomean kizomba music is very similar to the Angolan, Juka being the most notable among the Sãotomeans, and also one of the most notable performers in the genre.

steps.action: Several individuals with a love of the Kizomba c
steps.text: Several individuals with a love of the Kizomba culture have been promoting it in other countries.

steps.action: In an interview Key said she sought originality
steps.text: In an interview Key said she sought originality and new styles: "I'm running this responsibility of being predictable.

steps.action: I wanted to record Kizomba for 13 years!
steps.text: I wanted to record Kizomba for 13 years!

steps.action: Now I feel mature and have knowledge of movement
steps.text: Now I feel mature and have knowledge of movement".
